content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration · · · Executive Legal · · · Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5901          322-6441

March 26, 1976

Mr. A. Alan Post
Legislative Analyst
925 L Street, Suite 650
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Post:

     This is in response to your letter of March 4, and
also to confirm Michael Bennett's conversation last week
with Keith Winnard of your office.  Mr. Bennett provided
Mr. Winnard with all the Commission regulations relating
to lobbying and campaign disclosure statements.

     As Mr. Bennett explained, the Commission has not
yet established procedures to evaluate the Franchise Tax
Board's audits since we only received the first audits
from the Board a few weeks ago.  The Board emphasized to
us that these audits are not to be construed as typical
audits since they are the very first ones performed.
However, the audits we have received appear to be very
thorough.

     During 1975, Rudy Nothenberg, who is an accountant,
acted as liaison between the Board and our office.  He
and other members of our staff met with the Board's
auditing division and answered various questions about
the Political Reform Act.  Nothenberg is no longer with
the Commission, but we are about to borrow the services
of an accountant from the Board who has been an auditor
in their Political Reform Unit.  We expect this person
to provide a valuable link between the Franchise Tax
Board and the Commission.

     We have been meeting with the Board on their audits
and soon will be establishing a CPA Task Force to assist
us and the Board in developing regulations and procedures
in this area.  We expect to adopt regulations which will
make clear that a "standard of materiality" is to be
applied to the audits.


Mr. A. Alan Post
March 26, 1976
Page Two


     Finally, we are examining whether or not random
audits of lobbyists should occur rather than audits of
every lobbyist. This proposal would have to be made in
the legislative process rather than through regulations.

     We anticipate that as the initial round of audits
are concluded and made available to us that we and the
Board will be in an excellent position to examine audit-
ing procedures and methods.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                        Chairman

DHL:ejb


[illegible]
